channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Scammers try to trick LastPass users into giving up credentials by telling them they’re dead
item.description: Are you sure you’re still alive? If so, you may fall for a phishing scam aimed at getting the master login passwords of LastPass password manager users. OK, this sounds weird, but in some ways it isn’t.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why businesses banning AI inevitably lose
item.description: Those AI tools are being trained on our trade secrets. We’ll lose all of our customers if they find out our teams use AI. Our employees will no longer be able to think critically because of the brain rot caused by overreliance on AI. These are not irrational fears.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Klyagin, Fast Company

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Personas y tecnología, un binomio ganador en ING
item.description: Solo es posible alcanzar el éxito en la transformación digital empresarial si ésta va acompañada de una sólida cultura basada en las personas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Shifting your organizational focus from MTTD to MTTC in 8 steps
item.description: I spend a lot of time with operations leaders who have excellent visibility … but disappointing outcomes. The paradigm is that we invest heavily in detection, correlation and dashboards, then depend on human heroics. It’s the smoke-alarm-without-a-sprinkler problem.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: EU accuses Meta and TikTok of breaching transparency rules
item.description: The European Union says Meta and TikTok have breached transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mcneil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI: Latest news and insights
item.description: OpenAI is an artificial intelligence organization comprised of the non-profit OpenAI, Inc. and several for-profit subsidiaries. The company is perhaps best known for its ChatGPT chatbot, which launched in 2022, kicking off a period of massive disruption in the tech industry and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs, be ready for agentic AI
item.description: CIOs are staring down a paradox. Employees are moving faster than ever with agentic AI. They’re using tools like Copilot, ChatGPT and embedded SaaS agents to build automations and streamline workflows on their own.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI ‘workslop’: The new productivity killer only training can stop
item.description: AI offers workers the promise of increased efficiency and productivity, freeing them up from repetitive work to tackle more complex tasks. But as companies have rolled out AI tools to employees, many are facing a different challenge: AI-generated work that does the opposite.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The more corporate IT leaders, the greater the need to coexist
item.description: On a global average, CIOs hold 1.8 different positions, according to Foundry’s State of the CIO 2025 survey. So while some see themselves strictly as CIOs, others take on CTO, CISO, chief innovation officer, or COO duties.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bethesda celebrates Fallout Day with a host of announcements, including a Fallout 4 Anniversary Edition and Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, a native Fallout 76 release for PS5 and Xbox Series X, and more
item.description: Bethesda celebrates Fallout Day with a slew of announcements that include a Fallout 4 Anniversary Edition, Fallout 76 for current-gen consoles, and more.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Nottingham Forest vs FC Porto: Europa League 2025/26 free stream, TV channels, kick-off time
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch Nottingham Forest vs FC Porto as Sean Dyche looks to secure the East Midlands side the first win in the competition.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Maccabi Tel Aviv vs Midtjylland: Europa League 2025/26 live stream, TV channels, kick-off time
item.description: We'll show you all the ways to watch Maccabi Tel Aviv vs Midtjylland with the Israeli side looking to focus on what they can do on the pitch.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: US considers new software export curbs on China, threatening global tech supply chains
item.description: The US government is reportedly considering new export controls that could block a wide range of products made with US software from being shipped to China, in what could become one of Washington’s most sweeping trade measures to date.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI chatbots are wrong about news 45% of the time, study finds
item.description: A new international study coordinated by the European Broadcasting Union (EBU) and led by the BBC shows that AI assistants distort news content nearly half of the time. The research included 22 public service broadcasters in 18 countries and 14 languages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agentic labor for the U.S. military: Turning paperwork into strategic advantage
item.description: Agentic AI can run a whole workflow with minimal human oversight. The U.S. military, with a workforce of 3 million people, could be a top beneficiary. The post Agentic labor for the U.S. military: Turning paperwork into strategic advantage first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes comprehensive ban on marrying AI systems and granting legal personhood
item.description: House Bill 469 would prevent AI systems from owning property, serving as executives, or gaining legal personhood in Ohio under Representative Thaddeus Claggett's proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Call to ban AI superintelligence could redraw the global tech race between the US and China
item.description: More than 850 prominent figures have called for a prohibition on developing AI superintelligence, a move that could reshape enterprise AI investments and intensify the US-China technology race if adopted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Self-propagating worm found in marketplaces for Visual Studio Code extensions
item.description: A month after a self-propagating worm was discovered in the open source NPM code repository, a similar worm has been found targeting Visual Studio Code extensions in open marketplaces.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Treasury CIO says IRS IT layoffs are ‘painful,’ but necessary for reorganization
item.description: Treasury CIO and DOGE representative Sam Corcos defended IRS layoffs as “painful” but necessary to reboot the agency’s stalled IT modernization efforts. The post Treasury CIO says IRS IT layoffs are ‘painful,’ but necessary for reorganization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: How to improve technical documentation with generative AI
item.description: Devops teams have a love-hate relationship with writing and consuming technical documentation. Developers loathe reading and maintaining undocumented code. Architecture diagrams can tell a great story, but much of it is fiction compared to the implemented architecture.
item.dc:creator: Info World
